purpose.summary: This COBOL program updates a pending authorization summary and inserts authorization details into an IMS database. It retrieves the current date and time, calculates adjusted date and time values, and populates the pending authorization details segment with data from the request and response messages.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger authorization processing system, handling the persistence of authorization requests and responses.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Contains summary information about pending authorizations for an account.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
inputs.description: Contains detailed information about a specific pending authorization.

inputs.name: XREF-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID from cross-reference data.

inputs.name: XREF-CUST-ID
inputs.description: Customer ID from cross-reference data.

inputs.name: ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT
inputs.description: Credit limit for the account.

inputs.name: ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT
inputs.description: Cash credit limit for the account.

inputs.name: WS-APPROVED-AMT
inputs.description: The approved amount for the authorization.

inputs.name: PA-TRANSACTION-AMT
inputs.description: Transaction amount from the pending authorization.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-AUTH-DATE
inputs.description: Authorization date from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-AUTH-TIME
inputs.description: Authorization time from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-CARD-NUM
inputs.description: Card number from the authorization request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-AUTH-TYPE
inputs.description: Authorization type from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-CARD-EXPIRY-DATE
inputs.description: Card expiry date from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MESSAGE-TYPE
inputs.description: Message type from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MESSAGE-SOURCE
inputs.description: Message source from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-PROCESSING-CODE
inputs.description: Processing code from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-TRANSACTION-AMT
inputs.description: Transaction amount from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-CATAGORY-CODE
inputs.description: Merchant category code from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-ACQR-COUNTRY-CODE
inputs.description: Acquirer country code from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-POS-ENTRY-MODE
inputs.description: POS entry mode from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-ID
inputs.description: Merchant ID from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-NAME
inputs.description: Merchant name from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-CITY
inputs.description: Merchant city from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-STATE
inputs.description: Merchant state from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-MERCHANT-ZIP
inputs.description: Merchant zip code from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RQ-TRANSACTION-ID
inputs.description: Transaction ID from the request.

inputs.name: PA-RL-AUTH-ID-CODE
inputs.description: Authorization ID code from the response.

inputs.name: PA-RL-AUTH-RESP-CODE
inputs.description: Authorization response code from the response.

inputs.name: PA-RL-AUTH-RESP-REASON
inputs.description: Authorization response reason from the response.

inputs.name: PA-RL-APPROVED-AMT
inputs.description: Approved amount from the response.

inputs.name: IMS-PSB-SCHD
inputs.description: Indicates if the IMS PSB is scheduled.

inputs.name: W01-HCONN-REQUEST
inputs.description: MQSeries connection handle for the request queue.

inputs.name: W01-HOBJ-REQUEST
inputs.description: MQSeries object handle for the request queue.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
outputs.description: Updated summary information about pending authorizations.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: Detailed information about a specific pending authorization.

outputs.name: MQ Request Queue
outputs.description: Closes the MQ Request Queue if it is open.

business_rules.description: If a pending authorization summary segment is not found, initialize it with zeros and move account and customer IDs.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks NFOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG flag. If true, initializes PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY and moves XREF-ACCT-ID and XREF-CUST-ID to corresponding fields in the summary segment.

business_rules.description: Update the pending authorization summary based on the authorization response. If approved, increment the approved authorization count and amount. Otherwise, increment the declined authorization count and amount.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks AUTH-RESP-APPROVED flag. If true, increments PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT and adds WS-APPROVED-AMT to PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT and PA-CREDIT-BALANCE. Otherwise, increments PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT and adds PA-TRANSACTION-AMT to PA-DECLINED-AUTH-AMT.

business_rules.description: Set match flags based on authorization response. If approved, set PA-MATCH-PENDING to true. Otherwise, set PA-MATCH-AUTH-DECLINED to true.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks AUTH-RESP-APPROVED flag. If true, sets PA-MATCH-PENDING to true. Otherwise, sets PA-MATCH-AUTH-DECLINED to true.

transforms.transformation_description: Extracts the year and day from WS-CUR-DATE-X6.
Moves the current time to a numeric field.
Calculates the authorization date by subtracting WS-YYDDD from 99999.
Calculates the authorization time by subtracting WS-TIME-WITH-MS from 999999999.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is responsible for writing authorization information to the database. It orchestrates the update of the pending authorization summary and the insertion of the authorization details. It first performs 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY to update the summary information in the IMS database. Then, it performs 8500-INSERT-AUTH to insert the detailed authorization information into the IMS database. The paragraph does not directly handle any data manipulation or error conditions, but relies on the called paragraphs to perform these tasks. The purpose of this paragraph is to ensure both the summary and detailed information are written to the database.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 8000-WRITE-AUTH-TO-DB paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization summary in the IMS database. It first checks if a summary segment already exists. If not (NFOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG is true), it initializes the PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY segment with zeros and moves the account and customer IDs (XREF-ACCT-ID, XREF-CUST-ID) into the segment. It then moves the account credit limits (ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT, ACCT-CASH-CREDIT-LIMIT) into the summary segment. Based on whether the authorization was approved (AUTH-RESP-APPROVED), it increments the appropriate counters (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT or PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT) and amounts (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT or PA-DECLINED-AUTH-AMT) in the summary segment. Finally, it either updates an existing summary segment (if FOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG is true) or inserts a new one into the IMS database using EXEC DLI REPL or EXEC DLI ISRT, respectively. If the IMS operation fails, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 8400-UPDATE-SUMMARY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point.

This paragraph inserts the pending authorization details into the IMS database. It first retrieves the current date and time using CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands, storing the values in WS-CUR-DATE-X6 and WS-CUR-TIME-X6. It then calculates adjusted date and time values (PA-AUTH-DATE-9C, PA-AUTH-TIME-9C) by subtracting the current date and time from a constant. The paragraph moves various fields from the authorization request (PA-RQ-*) and response (PA-RL-*) into the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. Based on the authorization response (AUTH-RESP-APPROVED), it sets either PA-MATCH-PENDING or PA-MATCH-AUTH-DECLINED to TRUE. It then inserts the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment into the IMS database using EXEC DLI ISRT. If the IMS operation fails, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 8500-INSERT-AUTH paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point.

This paragraph performs termination procedures for the program. It first checks if the IMS PSB is scheduled (IMS-PSB-SCHD). If so, it terminates the PSB using EXEC DLI TERM. Then, it performs 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE to close the MQSeries request queue. This ensures that all resources are released before the program ends.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point.

This paragraph closes the MQSeries request queue. It first checks if the request queue is open (WS-REQUEST-MQ-OPEN). If so, it calls the MQCLOSE program to close the queue, passing the connection handle (W01-HCONN-REQUEST), object handle (W01-HOBJ-REQUEST), and other parameters. After the MQCLOSE call, it checks the completion code (WS-COMPCODE). If the completion code is not MQCC-OK, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR, indicating that the attempt to close the request queue failed. If the close is successful, WS-REQUEST-MQ-CLSE is set to TRUE.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9100-CLOSE-REQUEST-QUEUE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which allows control to return to the calling paragraph. It does not perform any data manipulation, error handling, or call any other paragraphs. Its sole purpose is to provide a standard exit point.

error_handling.action: MOVE 'I003' TO ERR-LOCATION, SET ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-IMS to TRUE, MOVE IMS-RETURN-CODE to ERR-CODE-1, MOVE 'IMS UPDATE SUMRY FAILED' to ERR-MESSAGE, MOVE PA-CARD-NUM to ERR-EVENT-KEY, PERFORM 9500-LOG-ERROR

MOVE 'I004' TO ERR-LOCATION, SET ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-IMS to TRUE, MOVE IMS-RETURN-CODE to ERR-CODE-1, MOVE 'IMS INSERT DETL FAILED' to ERR-MESSAGE, MOVE PA-CARD-NUM to ERR-EVENT-KEY, PERFORM 9500-LOG-ERROR

MOVE 'M005' TO ERR-LOCATION, SET ERR-WARNING and ERR-MQ to TRUE, MOVE WS-COMPCODE to WS-CODE-DISPLAY, MOVE WS-CODE-DISPLAY to ERR-CODE-1, MOVE WS-REASON to WS-CODE-DISPLAY, MOVE WS-CODE-DISPLAY to ERR-CODE-2, MOVE 'FAILED TO CLOSE REQUEST MQ' to ERR-MESSAGE, PERFORM 9500-LOG-ERROR

cics_operations.purpose: Formats the absolute time into year, day, time, and milliseconds.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of subtracting the current date and time from a constant to calculate PA-AUTH-DATE-9C and PA-AUTH-TIME-9C?
open_questions.context: The code calculates PA-AUTH-DATE-9C and PA-AUTH-TIME-9C by subtracting WS-YYDDD and WS-TIME-WITH-MS from constants. The reason for this transformation is unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Review the system documentation or consult with the developers to understand the purpose of this date/time transformation.

open_questions.question: What is the structure and purpose of the ERR-* fields and the 9500-LOG-ERROR paragraph?
open_questions.context: The code uses ERR-* fields to store error information and calls 9500-LOG-ERROR to handle errors. The exact structure and purpose of these elements are unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Review the program's copybooks and related documentation to understand the error handling mechanism.
